posts.author: Jason_Castelino
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — Finally found someone who believes in efficient market theory.

posts.author: Jason_Castelino
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — Because you are doing exactly same as what others are doing. Now can everybody make money ? Fno is a zero sum game. So if you do the same thing that others are doing, then you will end up only in losses like most others. Do something different.

posts.author: Rits
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — Trading is more than charts, candles,indicators, strategies, profit or loss. Its about discipline, patience, market understanding & above all behaviour psychology. Retailors will always loose money not because of lack of skills or charts but due to emotions & poor mindset.

posts.author: Shubhanshu
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — Because most traders don’t actually trade with logic. They trade with instinct first - fear, greed, hope, ego, FOMO. Then they use logic to justify the decision. Most traders don’t lose because they lack knowledge or strategy. They lose because instinct controls execution, and overtrading compounds the damage. x.com Nithin Kamath (@Nithin0dha) on X @Nithin0dha The biggest reason active traders lose money is overtrading, the low brokerage doesn't help. Traders forget that costs like STT, stamp duty, etc. are charged as a % of every trade & compound quickly.…

posts.author: Ranjit_DailyTrader
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — Trading is easy to learn, hard to survive in. Charts and indicators don’t matter if your emotions control your trades. Most people lose in F&O because of greed, revenge trading, and overconfidence. One bad trade can wipe out weeks of profits.

posts.author: Adarsh_Patil
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — We all have our own takes on this @Funancee . I was reading Professor Andrew Lo’s Adaptive Markets Hypothesis the other day it clicked for me. The market isn’t a math problem; it’s a living thing. The catch is that the second you spot a pattern, it’s already dying. As soon as enough people jump on it, that edge is hunted to extinction. If you follow that logic to the end, you get total randomness. That’s all an efficient market really is, not some perfect system, but a graveyard of patterns that have already been eaten. If the market doesn’t feel random, i…

posts.author: Meher_Smaran
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — Just like any other business, Trading also has only few winners, who have enough resources, patience and strategy in place. Excess greed and fear just amplifies the losses more and trims gains. Lose small (Having a proper risk mgmt system in place) and letting winners run…is something most traders miss out doing and hence, the painful journey PS: Let the trade come to us…not the other way around. Overtrading Kills.

posts.author: Utkarsh_S
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — It’s not that hard—just two things: understand basic fundamental analysis and stay up to date with the news. For the latter, I built a free tool that helps monitor news and trace the causal path from event → sector → specific NSE stocks. Maybe don’t use it for day trading. Instead, read the news and understand why it would impact a stock—like an upcoming trade pact or similar developments. You can also do a deep dive into stocks there to analyze their fundamentals. signal-in.vercel.app signal.in — Geopolitical Intelligence for Indian Equity Investors Real-…

posts.author: Dailytrader
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — Trading is not easy because the market is not about just charts, indicators, strategies, and patterns; it is also about psychology, risk management, patience, and discipline. If you face loss, then you don’t control your emotions and take the wrong trade to recover the loss, it’s a revenge trading, and you face more losses. Everyone wants to make money in the “short term” Set a proper “SL and TP”. Do not follow the chart pattern and indicator only; follow the market behaviour and volatility as well. Fear, greed, revenge trading, and overconfidence often le…

posts.author: siva
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — I think one of the reasons is trading with gut feeling than with any proper plan. Revenge trading which leads to over trading.

posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — @Meher_Smaran @siva @Adarsh_Patil @nithin_kumrr @Shubhanshu Your views on this?

posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — In addition to what @dgen and @BB789 - I would say what you quoted are also reasons why it is so hard Majority of retailers also dont know how to backtest the right way. Great backtest results are often ‘bad’ in live, infact backtest can more often than not be counter-intuitive, if one is looking for directional edge.

posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — It’s rigged

posts.author: niftymonk
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — People who need money in short term (retailers) always lose from people who don’t need money in short term (Big players). When in need, people take leverage and take dumb decusions and big players take advantage of retailers desperation.

posts.author: dgen
posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — For the most part because it is a losing proposition. Similar to a casino, the odds aren’t in your favour.

posts.text: Why Trading Is Harder Than It Looks — Why is trading so hard even after learning charts, indicators, and strategies for years? And why do most people still lose money in F&O?

posts.text: Trading groups in Hyderabad — We could meet if you like. Anyone from hyd. @VijayNair @shivak @Prakashsingh

posts.author: pavinjoseph
posts.text: Trading strategies reality — Okay, I’ll bite even though it reads like an LLM. Professional CTAs use systematic algorithmic trading all the time. Sometimes it’s as simple as a moving average crossover, their algos buy/sell regardless of news or whatever else is happening based on price/volume (and indicators) alone.

posts.author: secureputcallsusa
posts.text: Trading strategies reality — It’s actually a mix of both, but big traders don’t rely on fixed strategies like EMA crossovers or inside bars alone. They focus more on “**price action, market structure, and risk management”, and use indicators or patterns just for confirmation. Simple setups can work, but without proper context and discipline, they don’t last.

posts.author: Ranjit_DailyTrader
posts.text: Trading strategies reality — Big traders don’t rely on fixed strategies like inside bars or EMA crossovers. They use them as tools, not rules. What they really focus on is: market structure, liquidity, trader behavior A pattern is just a surface signal. They care about why it’s happening. Beginners follow patterns. Professionals read the market behind them.

posts.author: Devendra_Pachpor
posts.text: Trading is gambling or not : An interesting discussion — Trading becomes gambling when the participant lacks a disciplined system. This is often called “speculating” or “revenge trading.” Signs include: Emotional Decisions: Buying because of FOMO (Fear Of Missing Out). Lack of Risk Management: Risking the entire account on one “sure thing.” No Strategy: Entering the market based on “gut feelings” rather than technical or fundamental analysis.

posts.author: Viki_Vignesh
posts.text: Trading is gambling or not : An interesting discussion — Risk to Reward is the name of the Game. Even if your odds of winning are less than 50 percent, if you lose less when you lose and if you win big when you win; the odds will be stacked in your favor
